summary: Woodward 说，Gemini 正在变成一台刷题机器；Albert 把 Claude Code 叫作整个 workspace；Cat Wu 则做出了一个桌面控制中心，支持并行 sessions 和 review 工具。Rauch 还提到，agent builder 需要的是 elastic Postgres，不是靠感觉。

builderInsights.title: Josh Woodward
builderInsights.suggestedTitle: Gemini 正在变成一台备考机器
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: Gemini 现在已经支持印度最大医学考试 NEET 的练习测试，后面还会覆盖更多科目和国家。这很明显是在释放一个信号：Google 想让 Gemini 真正帮上高风险备考的忙，而不只是拿来聊天。

tweets.text:
New in Gemini: NEET practice tests! 🇮🇳🩺

Sending good luck to everyone taking India's biggest medical exam in a few weeks. You've got this!

We’re working on adding more practice tests for different subjects and countries. What should we add next? Let us know below 👇 https://t.co/EnCMI4Opdz

builderInsights.title: Alex Albert
builderInsights.suggestedTitle: Claude Code 正在变成整个工作区
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: 他说，新的 Claude Code 桌面 app 带来了一堆功能和性能升级。现在他做大多数工作时，几乎已经不需要别的 app，甚至连 terminal 都很少用了。这是个很强的信号：Anthropic 正在把 Claude 从 chatbot 往完整的工作界面上推。

tweets.text:
New version of Claude Code in the desktop app dropped today with tons of new features and performance improvements. 

Between Cowork and Code, I've found I don't really need to open other apps (or even my terminal) to do most of my work now. https://t.co/0HuRKunqzx

builderInsights.authorBio: ceo @box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
builderInsights.title: Aaron Levie
builderInsights.suggestedTitle: AI agent 需要服务，不只是 software
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: 他认为，enterprise AI agent 不会干掉 forward-deployed engineer，反而会让这个角色更重要。卖 agent，本质上是在卖一套 workflow。这就意味着 vendor 不能只会卖产品，还得懂 domain、会做 system wiring，还要能推进 change management。不然 adoption 很容易卡住。

tweets.text:
One corollary to the fact that AI agents take real work to setup in company at scale, is that the role of the forward deployed engineer -or whatever it gets called in the future- isn’t going away any time soon. 

When a vendor sells any kind of agents into an organization, you’re no longer just selling a software tool that gets implemented and you’re done. You’re fundamentally selling some form of the actual workflow being done by your technology. This is far closer to a customer buying from a professional services firm than implementing traditional technology.

This will almost always require a deep understanding of the domain that the customer operates in, the ability to help a customer wire up their systems to support the agents, make sure all the context is setup in the right way, and help provide change management to actually get the company to adapt its business processes.

The ability to do this across customers, figure out best practices in a specific industry and customer segment, take new features back to go build in the product, and so on is going to be key. There’s no shortcut to getting this work done by the enterprise, and the vendors are going to have to do a lot of this or risk low adoption.

Finally, this is a big opportunity for existing and next gen professional services companies. There are all new practice areas emerging in every system integrator and consulting firm just to do this kind of work, and this is going to continue to be in demand for quite some time. Yet another example of jobs that aren’t actually going away.

builderInsights.title: Cat Wu
builderInsights.suggestedTitle: Claude Code 变成了桌面控制中心
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: 现在，桌面版 Claude Code 成了同时管理本地和云端多个 session 的地方。它还支持 git status、固定运行结果，以及拖拽式布局。她还提到，Routines 可以按计划、通过 GitHub events，或者借助其他 API 来启动 Claude Code。说白了，它不只是个聊天框，而是在往 automation layer 走。

tweets.text:
Over the past few weeks, I’ve become a daily user of Claude Code on desktop.

It’s the best way to kick off and manage multiple CC sessions across local and cloud in one place. You can also see git status, pin active sessions, and drag and drop layout to view multiple sessions at the same time!

Let us know what you think :)

Automate the manual repetitive parts of your work with Routines! 

You can now kick off Claude Code on a schedule, from GH events, and from other APIs https://t.co/cmb3zr53Jk

builderInsights.authorBio: Builder. Dangerously skips permissions. Harvard’17. GitHub: https://t.co/KCuEajezlL YouTube: https://t.co/8xzbGWtf6w
builderInsights.title: Zara Zhang
builderInsights.suggestedTitle: AI 只有不断删减，才能更快做出来
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: 她说，Tab Out 真正的工作不是加功能，而是删功能。这个项目一开始是个重度依赖 LLM 的 tab manager，后来被砍成了一个纯 Chrome extension，数据也全部本地存储。因为真正有用的部分，其实只是把 tabs 看清楚，然后快速关掉。她的结论是：如果不拴住 Claude，它很容易一路给你 overbuild。

tweets.text:
How I came up with this idea:
1. I first realized all Chrome browsing history is saved locally in a database and can be parsed by LLM; so I was like: what can I build with this?
2. I tried to build an extension that surfaces insights based on browser history. But I realized if I had to click a button to bring it up every time, I'm never gonna click it. I need an entry-point that I'm already visiting frequently
3. I asked Claude, can you turn Chrome's New Tab page into the entry point? To my surprise it did just that. This is how I realized you can actually customize the New Tab page!
4. I built a page that displays all open tabs and makes an API call to an LLM to group them into "missions", so if a mission is complete, you can close it out. But that made it slow & not super reliable
5. I cut down the LLM feature; realized the most important thing is display tabs clearly  + closing them. Also cut down 5 other things I didn't need
Code: https://t.co/VpLLq4W2Yt

Ok I have simplified Tab Out even further:
- Tab Out is now a pure Chrome extension. No server, no Node.js, no npm
- Saved tabs are stored in https://t.co/ggaSm4eBVR.local; 100% local

For this little app I spent 20% of the time building features and 80% of the time cutting features. Claude tends to overkill; you need to keep it in check

builderInsights.authorBio: partner @fpvventures - investing in seed/A. previous: early hire @meter, @opendoor, @atlassian & others. love @shimoleejhaveri + 👦👧
builderInsights.title: Nikunj Kothari
builderInsights.suggestedTitle: vibe coding 救不了复杂系统
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: 他认为，简单的 workflow 很快就会被 vibe-coded 掉，但真正的系统还是得靠时间、耐心和一堆脏活累活。Slack 的通知系统就是个例子：你可以很快做出 prototype，但质量和深度还是得一点点磨出来。

tweets.text:
Every time I see a tweet saying “I can vibe code this in a weekend” - I think of the slack notification system..

It takes time, persistence and effort to get the details right. 

Sure, a lot of simple workflows will get vibe coded away. And maybe you can put this in Claude Code and get the code right in one shot. 

But quality, depth and great systems will still have value and take time. You can’t vibe code lessons. 

Now and forever.

Building an experimental new side project and need some beta testers with a specific criteria..

Parents with young kids looking to ACTIVELY buy a house in the US. 

Free to try right now, but will be paid given the sheer token costs 😅

builderInsights.title: Amjad Masad
builderInsights.suggestedTitle: AI 把一次性 app 变成了可复用的技能
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: 他说，过去他得自己临时拼出来的那些一次性 conversion app，现在已经变成了一项技能。这才是关键变化：AI 让 app 开发从一次性定制，变成了可重复的能力。 他还提到一个对印度 builders 很实用的 Replit 更新：现在可以直接用 Razorpay 来 build 和支付，少了一个很真实的摩擦点。

tweets.text: I built so many one-off conversion apps — now it’s just a skill. https://t.co/7MUWP3KyxK
Indian Replit Builders: You can now build & pay with @Razorpay https://t.co/rZNHDHQwTt

builderInsights.title: Guillermo Rauch
builderInsights.suggestedTitle: Agent builder 需要弹性更强的 Postgres，不是靠感觉
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: 他说，任何在做 agent coding platform 的人，都该把 app generation 和一个高弹性、兼容 Postgres 的 database 配在一起。这话听起来像是在轻轻带货，但意思很明确：如果 AI 要快速起 app，backend 也得跟得上。Vercel 的 DSQL 就是他拿来举例的那个。

tweets.text:
If you're thinking about making an agent coding platform, this is timely…

You'll want to pair app generations with a database service that's highly elastic. And Postgres-compatible. Like DSQL:
https://t.co/1zU4cpWREZ

builderInsights.authorBio: VC at @FirstMarkCap.  Host: MAD Podcast; Organizer: Data Driven NYC, Author: MAD Landscape.
builderInsights.title: Matt Turck
builderInsights.suggestedTitle: AI 让 VC 的建议越来越同质化
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: 他开玩笑说，AI 给每个 VC 的投资建议都差不多。问题也正出在这儿：大家越是依赖同一套工具，羊群效应就越容易冒出来。FirstMark VC 和 MAD Landscape 作者 Matt Turck 也在提醒大家：现在想证明“独立思考”更难了，不是更容易了。

tweets.text:
The good news is that, while AI is also giving every VC the same investment advice, we're such independent thinkers that there is no risk of herd thinking 

(great watch 👇) https://t.co/1EeU2PqONn

builderInsights.title: Nan Yu
builderInsights.suggestedTitle: 大公司失手很正常——看赢面，不要只盯着输面
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: 他说，大家对 Google 和 Meta 的失手反应过度了，因为它们的执行体量太大。对 Linear 这样的产品负责人来说，更该看的不是那些显眼的失败，而是这种规模带来的胜利次数。

tweets.text:
Every time someone shits on Google or Meta they need to be reminded that those companies are unfathomably large. 

Their misses are going to seem enormous because their basic unit of execution is huge. 

Like in most businesses: count wins not losses

builderInsights.authorBio: President & CEO @ycombinator —Founder @garryslist—Creator of GStack & GBrain—designer/engineer who helps founders—SF Dem accelerating the boom loop

While our Asian American communities watch in stunned silence as our elders are murdered with no accountability.

builderInsights.authorBio: I share extremely practical AI tutorials and interviews | Join 140K+ readers at https://t.co/XYKTmGVH14 | Product at Roblox
builderInsights.title: Peter Yang
builderInsights.suggestedTitle: AI 让你很快到平均水平；taste 才能把它做得更好
builderInsights.suggestedSummary: 他说，AI 的输出不是终点，而是原材料。和 Dylan 聊的时候，他想表达的是：agent 可以生成无穷多种方案，但你还是得自己判断、打磨，再靠 taste 把它从平均水平往上拉。这对用 Claude Code 或类似工具的人很有用：model 负责加速探索，但最后值不值得留，还是得人来定。

tweets.text: Why is it still asking me for perms after I turned on bypass perms and always accept edits 🤔 https://t.co/K15JEi8JjY

I'm enjoying using the new Claude Code app (RIP terminal). 

How can I work on the same session across the Claude mobile app too? 

/remote-control doesn't seem to work and tbh I shouldn't have to type this to work across desktop + mobile anyway. https://t.co/TAh7ny1t6W https://t.co/GAXO9RGsfR

"It's a mistake to think of the output of a prompt as a final result."

Here's @zoink on the difference between crafting with AI vs. outsourcing your thinking:

"When you're exploring divergent possibilities with agents, you need to take [AI's output] and mold and shape it like clay.

Ultimately, you're the judge of the system. You're the one that needs to figure out what is good and worth exploring because the possibilities are likely almost infinite."

tl:dr AI gets you to average fast. Your taste is what pushes past it.

📌 Watch my full chat with Dylan here: https://t.co/gaYK2S2cRW

podcastHighlights.title: 从 SEO 到 Agent 驱动增长：ProFound 的 James Cadwallader
podcastHighlights.suggestedTitle: 营销现在得说服 agent，而不只是人
podcastHighlights.suggestedSummary:
**核心结论：** 营销正在从“给人看、给人排位”转向“让 agent 能读懂、能用、能引用”。

- agent 不只是总结网页。它们抓取的范围比人广得多，所以长尾内容比过去那套 top-3 SEO 游戏更重要。
- 最大的误区，是把这件事当成 SEO 2.0。现在内容要先被 AI 发现和消费，而不只是被人类索引到。
- 不同平台差异很明显：Gemini 更依赖 YouTube，ChatGPT 经常拉 Reddit 或 LinkedIn，Claude 也在越来越“吃网页”。

ProFound 联合创始人兼 CEO James Cadwallader 认为，营销已经进入了一个新的 operating system。他的公司帮助品牌理解自己在 ChatGPT、Claude、Gemini 和 Grok 这类模型里的 AI search 和 agentic answers 中是怎么被呈现的。他的核心观点很直接：营销史上“最大的一次 platform shift”已经开始了，因为消费者正在被 agent 取代，购买旅程由 agent 代为完成。

这会直接改变工作方式。过去，marketers 优化的是人类注意力和前几个蓝色链接。现在，Cadwallader 说，agent 会用“数量级更大”的 surface area，有时一次 query 会拉取几十个页面。这意味着品牌需要的内容，不只是关键词够多，而是要让模型一眼就能读懂。尤其在 software 领域，他还会进一步看 usability 和 interoperability：agent 能不能真的帮你排障、对比、推荐你的产品？

他也不认同“AI content 天生就是垃圾”这种说法。在他看来，真正的限制是 originality。如果模型已经知道互联网上的一切，marketers 就得给它们一些它们不知道的东西。或者用他的话说，人类现在是“现实和 Internet 之间一个有血有肉的 API”。

blogUpdates.title: 为并行 agent 重做桌面端 Claude Code
blogUpdates.suggestedTitle: Claude Code 桌面端新增并行 session 和内置 review 工具
blogUpdates.suggestedSummary: 导语：Claude Code 重新设计后的桌面 app，专门面向并行 agent 工作流。它加了一个新的侧边栏来管理多个 session，还内置了 review 和交付工具，不用离开 app 就能把工作做完。
